title: Public evidence docs expose workstation-specific absolute paths
attacker_controlled_source: Public reader of repository/security evidence documentation; no runtime input required.
broken_control: Evidence sanitization allows local user-profile and .codex absolute paths in public-facing validation records.
impact: Low-grade environment disclosure: local username/workstation path conventions and internal Codex generated-image path can support recon or make public evidence less sanitized.

candidate_local_validation.method: Full-file read plus targeted rg for absolute paths and evidence wording.
candidate_local_validation.evidence: Matched user-profile paths in docs/evidence/implementation_evidence.md lines 5 and 35, and docs/validation/local_validation.md lines 12-14.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: No token, password, API key, or credential material observed.

attack_path_facts.security_effect: privacy/recon exposure, not code execution or data exfiltration
counterevidence: Affected paths are local filesystem paths, not reachable URLs.
No OpenAI affiliation, external approval, or production-security certification claim was found.

recommended_fix: Replace user-profile paths with <repo_root> or relative paths and describe the generated image as a local design reference without publishing the .codex absolute path.

title: OpenAI-facing wording overstates no dependency install/static-only posture while repo contains optional Python PDF tooling with third-party imports
attacker_controlled_source: OpenAI/community/public wording and audit-evidence consistency boundary; no runtime input required.
broken_control: Docs say static HTML/CSS/JavaScript only and no dependency install without clearly scoping that claim to the deployable runtime/dashboard.
impact: A reviewer or public reader may believe the entire repository is dependency-free/static-only, weakening the documentation/evidence boundary.

candidate_local_validation.method: Full-file read plus rg for dependency/static wording and Python imports.
candidate_local_validation.evidence: scripts/generate_profile_pdfs.py imports PIL, pypdf, reportlab and optionally pypdfium2; OpenAI-facing docs and threat model contain unqualified no dependency/static-only wording.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: No approval, affiliation, external deployment, secret exposure, or runtime unsafe DOM sink observed.

attack_path_facts.sink: OpenAI-facing markdown and threat model assumptions
attack_path_facts.security_effect: audit/claim drift; not code execution or data exfiltration
counterevidence: README scopes the runtime as static/local-first.
No package manager manifest was found.
The Python tooling is local optional tooling, not the deployable runtime.

recommended_fix: Revise wording to say the deployable runtime is static HTML/CSS/JavaScript with no backend/build dependency, while the repository also contains optional local PDF tooling that may require Python libraries if used.
